persona.ai_comfort: enthusiastic beginner — excited about AI but still figuring it out
persona.motivation: Genuinely curious where I stand. Saw John Maeda share this and want to try it.
persona.tech_beliefs: AI is amazing and I want to learn everything about it! I'm not sure exactly how it fits my workflow yet but I'm excited to find out. Every new tool is a learning opportunity.

reflection.overall_reflection: The assessment was engaging and playful at first, but I found some parts confusing, especially with the labeling and progress tracking. Overall, it was a fun experience that made me think more about how I use AI.
usability_issues: The optional information form on page 2 was unclear whether it was mandatory or not, causing hesitation.
Some button text was cut off, making it hard to read full descriptions (e.g., on page 10).
No clear progress indicator made it hard to know how many questions were left.
Navigation back and forth was not always intuitive, especially after going back to review previous answers.
reflection.nps_reason: I found the assessment genuinely valuable in helping me reflect on my AI usage, but some usability issues made it feel less polished. I would recommend it to others, especially if the interface is improved.
reflection.strongest_moment: The home page with the colorful buttons made me feel excited and curious about what was ahead — it was the most engaging and inviting part of the experience.
reflection.share_reason: I think the assessment is a good tool for people who are just starting to explore AI in their workflow, and I’d like to see others try it and get a sense of where they stand.
reflection.accessibility_note: The page structure was mostly clear, but the lack of progress indicators and truncated button text made it harder to navigate and understand.

sus.sus_notes: I found the system playful and engaging at first, but some parts felt confusing or overwhelming. I’m excited to keep learning, but I think it could be smoother for someone still figuring out AI workflows.
